title: Neurobiology: Resetting the axon's batteries.
author_string: Twiss, Jeffery L, Kalinski, Ashley L, Sahoo, Pabitra K, Costa, Irene Dalla, Giger, Roman J
abstract: Neuronal injury can cause mitochondrial damage, leading to reduced energy production, decreased Ca<sup>2+</sup> storage capacity, and increased reactive oxygen species. A new study reveals a mechanism to trigger the axonal transport of previously anchored mitochondria and promote neuroprotection and axon regeneration by replacing damaged with functional mitochondria.
